pattern: A number has alternating bits exactly when shifting it by one and XOR-ing creates an all-ones mask, which can be tested without inspecting bits one by one.
hints: Compare each bit to its neighbour by lining the number up against a one-bit shift of itself.
If bits alternate, XOR-ing the number with itself shifted right by one yields a run of consecutive ones.
Let m = n XOR (n >> 1); n alternates iff m has only set bits, which holds when m & (m + 1) equals zero.

edge_inputs.description: binary 1011 breaks pattern at the end

stress.description: random positive 31-bit integers covering alternating and non-alternating cases
